runtime_env.note: Append /no_think to Qwen prompts; apply --jinja --chat-template-file templates/qwen3_nonthinking.jinja and -no-cnv via llama-completion.

selection_reason: First canonical baseline. Direct-answer mode (Task 002C) fixes the Task 002B thinking-mode trap: no <think> block appears in any retest output, visible SME answers are produced within the token budget, and timing/memory fit the 8 GB target (generation ~4 tok/s, ~5.4 GB host memory on dev machine). Locked as a working baseline to unblock retrieval (Task 003); not a final endorsement (see unresolved_items).
unresolved_items: prompt-1 (triage) derailed into off-topic multiple-choice + chemistry hallucination even in direct mode; needs prompt/system-prompt hardening or a larger non-thinking model.
All TPS/memory numbers are from the development Darwin x86_64 machine, not the target Ubuntu 22.04 / 8 GB hardware.
Only qwen3-1.7b has been profiled; control (granite-4.1-3b) and reasoning fallback (qwen3-4b) candidates are still absent.
No checksum verification recorded yet for the candidate file.
prohibited_use: no cloud inference during judged runtime
